title: Κόραξ καὶ ἀλώπηξ
source: Émile Chambry, Ésope: Fables, texte établi et traduit (Les Belles Lettres, Paris, 1927), fable 165 (= Perry 124). Text as printed, including Chambry's editorial punctuation (« », ⟨ ⟩ for editorial supplements). Pronunciation: reconstructed Attic, after W. S. Allen, Vox Graeca. Romanization: standard classical transliteration (ē/ō; th/ph/ch; y for υ, u in diphthongs; h = rough breathing; ēi/āi/ōi for iota subscript; accents omitted).

sentences.t: Κόραξ κρέας ἁρπάσας ἐπί τινος δένδρου ἐκάθισεν.
sentences.tr: Korax kreas harpasas epi tinos dendrou ekathisen.
sentences.l: A crow, meat having-snatched, upon a certain tree perched.
sentences.i: A crow snatched a piece of meat and perched in a tree.
sentences.n: In the Greek the bird is a κόραξ, a raven/crow, and the prize is κρέας, meat — the famous cheese belongs to La Fontaine, not to the Greek tradition.

sentences.t: Ἀλώπηξ δὲ θεασαμένη αὐτὸν καὶ βουλομένη τοῦ κρέατος περιγενέσθαι στᾶσα ἐπῄνει αὐτὸν ὡς εὐμεγέθη τε καὶ καλόν, λέγουσα καὶ ὡς πρέπει αὐτῷ μάλιστα τῶν ὀρνέων βασιλεύειν, καὶ τοῦτο πάντως ἂν ἐγένετο, εἰ φωνὴν εἶχεν.
sentences.tr: Alōpēx de theasamenē auton kai boulomenē tou kreatos perigenesthai stasa epēinei auton hōs eumegethē te kai kalon, legousa kai hōs prepei autōi malista tōn orneōn basileuein, kai touto pantōs an egeneto, ei phōnēn eichen.
sentences.l: And a fox, having-caught-sight-of him and wishing of-the meat to-get-the-better, standing-there praised him as well-sized both and beautiful, saying also that it-befits him most of-the birds to-be-king, and this surely would have-happened, if a voice he-had.
sentences.i: A fox saw him, and wanting to get the meat for herself, stood there and praised him — so big, so handsome; no bird deserved more to be king of them all, and king he certainly would be, if only he had a voice.
sentences.n: περιγενέσθαι + genitive, 'get the better of' — the same predatory verb the fox aimed at the grapes in fable 32; here the target is the meat. The flattery ends in a contrary-to-fact sting: ἂν ἐγένετο, εἰ εἶχεν, 'it would have happened, if he had…' — the trap is built into the grammar.

sentences.t: Ὁ δὲ παραστῆσαι αὐτῇ θέλων ὅτι καὶ φωνὴν ἔχει, ἀποβαλὼν τὸ κρέας μεγάλα ἐκεκράγει.
sentences.tr: Ho de parastēsai autēi thelōn hoti kai phōnēn echei, apobalōn to kreas megala ekekragei.
sentences.l: And he, to-demonstrate to-her wishing that also a voice he-has, having-let-drop the meat, loudly screamed.
sentences.i: Wanting to show her that he had a voice too, he let the meat fall and gave a great caw.
sentences.n: μεγάλα, neuter plural used adverbially: 'loudly.' ἐκεκράγει is from κράζω — a properly harsh croak of a verb.

sentences.t: Ἐκείνη δὲ προσδραμοῦσα καὶ τὸ κρέας ἁρπάσασα ἔφη· « Ὦ κόραξ, καὶ φρένας εἰ εἶχες, οὐδὲν ἂν ἐδέησας εἰς τὸ πάντων σε βασιλεῦσαι. »
sentences.tr: Ekeinē de prosdramousa kai to kreas harpasasa ephē: « Ō korax, kai phrenas ei eiches, ouden an edeēsas eis to pantōn se basileusai. »
sentences.l: And she, having-run-up and the meat having-snatched, said: « O crow, wits too if you-had, nothing would you-have-lacked toward the over-all you being-king. »
sentences.i: The fox darted in, snatched up the meat, and said: « O crow — if you had brains as well, you would lack nothing to be king of all the birds. »
sentences.n: φρένες — the seat of wits; 'if you had phrenes' is exactly the fox's taunt to the goat in fable 40. The final flourish is an articular infinitive with its own subject: εἰς τὸ … σε βασιλεῦσαι, 'toward the you-being-king.'

sentences.t: Πρὸς ἄνδρα ἀνόητον ὁ λόγος εὔκαιρος.
sentences.tr: Pros andra anoēton ho logos eukairos.
sentences.l: Against a man foolish the story well-timed.
sentences.n: A second epimythium formula, terser than ὁ λόγος δηλοῖ: Πρὸς (person) ὁ λόγος εὔκαιρος, 'the story is timely against/for so-and-so.' You'll meet this one constantly too.
